items.title: Meta Mile
items.summary: You know how rappers have diss tracks? Where they battle their…
items.content_html:
You know how rappers have diss tracks?

Where they battle their fellow rapper foes via lyrical warfare?

I think we should do that at the corporate levels.

Diss tracks between F500 company CEOs.

I wouldn't buy 'em.

But I'd listen.

It doesn't even have to be rival C-Level quarrels.

It can be internal.

You gotta problem with Fred in HR?

Diss track.

He gotta problem with you?

Response track.

Loser leaves the company.

It ain't festivus.

But there is an airing of grievances.

Now, I realize this puts an awfully skewed amount of value towards rapping ability (likely not at all related to your occupation), but hey I don't make the rules.

I think corporate rap battles is the one thing missing from this silly world.

Of course there are (many, many) others, but I like to keep things simple.

But would you be shocked?

At all?

Even a little?

It kind of fits.

If you see Marky Zee on stage looking angry in a gi, ballcap turned to the side, and a mic in his hand.

With a beat on behind him that you don't wanna like, but kinda do.

You kinda do.

Well, what I can say except:

You're welcome.

items.title: Big and Small
items.summary: Quantum is one of my favorite words. I got a lot…
items.content_html:
Quantum is one of my favorite words.

I got a lot of them.

Actualization.

Starfaring.

Iterating.

But quantum's up there.

You want to know why?

Because it means two very different things at the same time.

It means opposite things simultaneously.

Quantum can be defined as something so small it's practically zero.

The notch right above nothing.

The smallest of the smallest.

At the same time, quantum can also refer to something very big.

Huge.

Significant.

This is not even to mention its use in physics, mechanics, computing.

It's the word de jour, a polarizing one to be sure (my favorites usually are), but it'll likely be an important word from here out.

Everything is measured.

Now more than ever.

May your life be quantum.

For the future, like the past, and the present, is.

0️⃣ and ♾

items.title: Songs of the Desert
items.summary: You know what's a good song? It's a poem, really. Like…
items.content_html:
You know what's a good song?

It's a poem, really.

Like all the best songs are.

Poetry.

I Hope You Dance.

I hope you never lose your sense of wonder.

I won't quote every line.

You get your fill to eat but always keep that hunger.

But I want to.

May you never take one single breath for granted.

Deep breath.

So good.

The songwriter wrote it by the ocean.

I hope you still feel small when you stand beside the ocean.

Which is where that line comes from.

🌊 .

Whenever one door closes, I hope one more opens.

🚪

Or two.

Three would be nice.

Promise me that you'll give faith a fighting chance.

Promise me.

A fighting chance.

I promise.

Be good, ya'll.

You know where I'll be.

💃🕺

items.title: Al Slop
items.summary: I'm testing out a new setup. Kind of like the old…
items.content_html:
I'm testing out a new setup.

Kind of like the old setup.

Writing on big monitors, instead of laptop ones.

Let's see how it works.

It occurs to me, as such things do, that I can't write when someone's over my shoulder.

Or in the room.

I don't know why I'm like that.

But I'm sure I'm not alone.

I need to be alone.

At least for the moment.

At least for the musings column.

So I can really exhale.

And get down to business.

So today, you get this.

It's not ai.

But it is kinda sloppy.

items.title: Amos
items.summary: You know a sure sign you're getting up there in age?…
items.content_html:
You know a sure sign you're getting up there in age?

You don't recognize a single person who is newly famous.

All these famous people walking around I ain't never heard of.

And it's got me wondering if they are really famous or just fed to us by some algorithm that makes people famous.

This person is famous. Please act accordingly.

But I repeat myself.

Fame, money, power.

Of the three, I always considered fame the worst.

It's an interesting thing.

I bet.

To be known to everyone. 

Or a lot of people.

But the people only know the "famous" you.

They don't know you you.

But they act like it.

They believe they do.

It would be strange to meet people who think they know you.

Fans who bother you while you're eating a quiet meal with your family.

I remember a good friend of mine and I shared a meal at a local diner.

Years ago.

Decades.

And we're just minding our business when I suddenly look over and see Jerry Seinfeld eating with another person.

"Is that Jerry Seinfeld?"

"Yes it is."

"Should we send him a slice of cheesecake?"

"Yes we should."

So we did.

Two punk kids.

We asked our waitress to offer Jerry a slice of cheesecake, compliments of the table across the way.

He took one look at us, waved, and politely declined.

We left him alone after that of course.

We're not monsters.

But we thought we were hilarious.

I mean, free cheesecake sounds like a nice fringe benefit of fame, but I know it only gets worse from there.

My daughter jokes, Dear Lord, I hope it's a joke, that she wants to be famous.

But she clarifies:

Not big famous, Daddy [holding her hands out as wide as she can].

Just a little famous [holding her hands a few inches apart].

Out of the mouth of babes...

🍪

To little famouses.

Hey! Ain't you that fella that writes the musings column on the internet? Iterating?

Ahh, thank you thank you.

No speakee Engrish.

You buy cheesecake?

items.title: Weal Is Me
items.summary: There are two ways to look at life. I'm sure there…
items.content_html:
There are two ways to look at life.

I'm sure there are more, but I tend to talk in polarities.

You can look at life with a "Woe is me" lens.

And boy, that sure fits the eyes, hmm?

👓

You know, you look at the world like the donkey on Pooh.

E-ho.

Does he mean Eeyore?

That's what I said.

E-ho.

And the other way to look at the world is "Dang, I'm one lucky mother mother."

🕶

Same stuff happens to you.

Same things in your pocket (even if just lint).

Same facts.

Wildly different outlook.

Why?

Because the innerlook is different.

Same outcomes.

Different insights.

I once sat in a room and listened to two people argue about who had the worst life.

They were fighting to "win" who had it the worst.

And I remember thinking, "Why would you want to win this one?"

Lord of the Wings.

Talk about an L in the shape of a W.

Why even be in contention?

The thing is, they didn't have a bad life.

They just saw it that way.

The people with the Woe lens tend to point at the obviously tragic things in their lives. 

To them, they stand out like monuments of trauma.

They drag them with them wherever they go.

But their index fingers brush past all the glory.

All the love.

All the good.

Standing by and looking up at the pointer.

Ignored.

Or at least forgotten.

Disregarded.

Taken for granted.

What about me? Why are you only focusing on the bad?

The people who realize how lucky they are brush past the monuments of trauma, are even strengthened by them, to focus on the blessings, however small.

They carry their sunshine, however little, in their pocket.

To focus on its golden linings.

🌞

A life-altering event?

Well, thank God I'm still here!

Thank you, God.

Thank goodness it wasn't worse!

That which dost not kill me.

Only.

Makes.

Me.

Stronger.

😎

"The final weapon is the brain. All else is supplemental."

Weapons, like fingers, like a lot of things really, all depend upon how and where you point them.

items.title: Graphically Novel
items.summary: I don't read comic books. But I did as a child.
items.content_html:
I don't read comic books.

But I did as a child.

And while I did enjoy reading various issues featuring the hero de jour, the one comic I looked forward to the most was called Marvel Universe.

It was an encyclopedia of the characters in the books.

Heroes.

Villains.

Cosmic entities too big to be either.

And everyone of note in between.

It was done alphabetically.

You'd get a letter or two in each issue. As. Bs. Cs to Ds. Etc.

Each entry would be a biography. Their strength level (I remember it topped out at pressing 100+ tons over their head). Their superpowers (if any).

This memory got me to thinking.

Uh oh.

Would anyone read the Actual Universe?

An encyclopedia of today's heroes, villains, cosmic entities too big to be either, and everyone of note in between?

I suppose social media is sort of like that.

Or personal websites.

But has anyone really categorized them and put them into one place?

Wikipedia?

But I can't pick up the As in Wiki.

Even I ain't that strong.

Oh boy, he's feeling it today.

This all falls into my theory that instead of resumes we should all have character sheets.

Instead of social media pages, we should have an Actual Universe handbook.

It occurs to me, as such things do, that a lot of the things we take for granted as truth, just isn't.

People lie.

Stretch the truth.

Remember it a certain way.

Asterisks.

I suppose a comic book doesn't fix that.

But it does frame it as true fiction.

And if you treated social media and personal websites as comic books, well things make a lot more sense.

I don't need your life story.

Well, yes I do.

Just keep it to a page.

Biography.

Strength level.

Superpowers (if any).

Sum up everything about you in a page or two.

And tell the truth, as you see it.

What do you share? 

What do you leave out?

What I've learned is, people just want to be entertained.

Especially in the comic book we call Life.

items.title: I Can Do Bad All By Myself
items.summary: Today, I want to talk about better bad decisions. Now, I'm…
items.content_html:
Today, I want to talk about better bad decisions.

Now, I'm not a health guru, but I play one on the internet.

No, you don't.

This one is for your own good.

Another one of my (few) rules.

If you're going to make a bad decision, make a better one.

A better bad decision.

For example.

You're craving a burger.

Juicy.

Fatty.

Plump.

🍔

So you don't go to the terrible spots.

You go to Steak 'n Shake.

They do use beef tallow.

And since you're here, you're thirsty.

So you get a Coke Zero instead of regular.

Zero calories.

But a ton of (acquired) taste.

Better, bad, decision.

You got me?

We still ordering large fries because my goodness, we're Americans.

🍟

Just don't eat them all.

I used to never order fries, making it a best bad decision. 

But I digest digress.

Split the meal into two meals.

Half burger, half fries, half drink.

Then you got another meal in two hours.

Better, bad, decisions.

I'm convinced that it's the "AND THEN"s that get folks into trouble.

Too many baddies stacked on top of one another.

Eating a bunch of 'em.

Your attitude should never be, "Might as well..."

It should be, "Well, I got my main bad, that's enough for me."

Ideally, no side bads.

You could apply this to all areas of life.

But let's stick to food here.

You can do bad all by yourself.

Just use my rule.

Trust me.

But you say not to trust you all the time.

People actually read this sheet?

Oh, and if you go to SnS, just tell 'em AL sent you.

I got a referral code here somewhere...

They'll most likely look at you funny (don't worry I'm used to that, you'll get used to it, too. Enjoy it, even.).

😒

Maybe kick you out.

"Who the heck is AL?"

But I'll be belly laughing.

They didn't even get the burger! AAAAHHHHHHHHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH.

items.title: Keep On
items.summary: When is the happiest you have ever been? As a stoic…
items.content_html:
When is the happiest you have ever been?

As a stoic curmudgeon, this is an interesting question to me.

And so I ask it of you.

When were you the happiest?

Brightest eyed?

Bushiest-taileded?

He made it an -est word for emphasis and added extra -ed at the end of tailed for (hopeful) comedic effect.

Is it now?

Or in childhood?

Or perhaps, as it could be, it has yet to happen?

Still though, the happiest moment thus far.

What was it?

What is it?

A single moment?

A single season?

A single run?

I will tell you, if you can keep a secret, that all of my happiest moments have occurred after I fell out.

After my nuclear fall out.

☢

After I almost touched God.

It's funny how you realize how trivial most things are once things get serious.

Having a come to Jesus moment, but hoping you don't get to meet the man Himself.

Just yet.

Just yet.

This truck's got a lot of miles on it.

But it's a good truck.

A lot of life left in it.

I hope.

Is he referring to himself as an automobile?

I hope.

But, if it's not the case, I want to tell you something.

The happiest I have ever been is right now.

This era.

This phase.

This iteration.

Just jipper.

So thankful for what has come, what will come, and what is.

Amen.

😋

Keep on, my fellow vehicle.

🚚

items.title: Even Me
items.summary: Can we keep it lighthearted, today? Let's do that. Keep it…
items.content_html:
Can we keep it lighthearted, today?

Let's do that.

Keep it light.

Hearted.

Cheerful.

Okay!

Have you ever trusted anyone to the level that one of them lil' flipping cheerleaders do?

I mean the ones that flip, fly, and soar and blindly trust that someone will (try to) catch them in mid-air?

I don't know why I just thought of that.

But it does require a level of trust that just seems, oh what's the word, silly.

Silly.

An asymmetric risk the other way around.

Low reward.

Moderate to high risk.

Terrible bet.

Of course, my favorite wisdom is DTA.

Don't.

Trust.

Anybody.

You see me flip flop flying in the air, don't try to catch me.

Trust me.

Something has obviously gone horribly wrong.

Let me handle it.

Let me land.

But you promised to keep it lighthearted!

Oh right.

Like a mother motherin' comic book character.

Fist down.

Head down.

Crater the ground.

Head up.

[The entire audience erupts in applause.]

Don't.

[Clap clap.]

Trust.

[Clap clap.]

Anyyyyyyy....

Body!!!

Yay!!!

🥂

Go Humans!!!

Woooooooo!!!!
